libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Do not directly instantiate DefaultSiteRenderer for rendering tests unless its injected fields are populated; in 2.0.0 it uses javax.inject fields for VelocityComponent, ParserModuleManager, Doxia, and PlexusContainer. Prefer looking up SiteRenderer/DefaultSiteRenderer from a Plexus/Sisu container available on the transitive classpath, or otherwise inject those collaborators deliberately in test code. No Docker image or environment/system property setup appears necessary.

deterministic_setup.reason: Upstream doxia-site-renderer tests add this parser module explicitly because Doxia modules are no longer all in doxia-core and their component descriptors must be on the test classpath for site document discovery/rendering.
XDoc rendering is a normal Doxia Site Renderer use case, but the parser module is test-scoped upstream and is not pulled transitively by doxia-site-renderer.
FML rendering is covered by upstream site-renderer tests, but the parser module is not a runtime transitive dependency of doxia-site-renderer.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The existing 1.9.1 tests use removed/changed APIs such as RenderingContext, SkinResourceLoader, enableLogging, and setUsingDefaultTemplate; setup alone will not fix the javac failure.

The public skin-related API references org.apache.maven.artifact.Artifact, which is a provided dependency upstream; tests that specifically exercise createContextForSkin or skin artifacts may need to avoid compile-time coupling or add a matching Maven artifact dependency during test implementation.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Meaningful site-rendering coverage for Doxia Site Renderer 2.0.0 normally relies on Plexus/Sisu component wiring and non-transitive Doxia parser modules; the artifact itself only brings xhtml5 at compile/runtime while apt, xdoc, and fml are test-scoped in the upstream project.
